items.q: What did Renaissance artists rediscover that medieval art lacked?
items.correct: Linear perspective and naturalistic representation of the human body.
items.why: Students assume medieval art was colourless. It was vivid but flat - the Renaissance breakthrough was mathematical PERSPECTIVE (Brunelleschi) and anatomical realism, not the introduction of colour.

items.q: What attitude does Pop Art take toward consumer culture?
items.correct: Ambiguous - it both celebrates and critiques mass media imagery.
items.why: Students read Pop Art as either pure celebration or pure protest. Warhol's deadpan repetition of Marilyn or soup cans is deliberately AMBIGUOUS, leaving viewers to decide if it's homage or critique.

items.q: What is appropriation in postmodern art?
items.correct: Deliberately reusing existing images or styles, often to comment on originality.
items.why: Students moralise about copying. Postmodern appropriation (Sherrie Levine, Richard Prince) is a CRITICAL STRATEGY - it questions authorship and originality, central modernist values.

items.q: LeWitt's 'Paragraphs on Conceptual Art' was published in:
items.why: Correct answer: 1967. “1957” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: The Sony Portapak became available in:
items.why: Correct answer: 1965. “1955” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Bill Viola lived 1951 to 2024. What was his age at death (in years)?
items.why: The real figure is 73. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
